refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve a measured p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, with cycle time performance monitored and reported via telemetry.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-002 is a key performance indicator (KPI) requirement that directly impacts customer productivity and system acceptance. Refinement adds explicit reference to measurement/monitoring (telemetry) and duty cycle context to make verification concrete. High safety criticality tailoring requires that cycle time optimization does not compromise safety interlocks or emergency response.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Instrumented field trials under representative duty cycle; p95 cycle time computed from telemetry logs; comparison against CT_target acceptance threshold; safety event correlation analysis to ensure no safety compromise.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-002.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within design envelope.
FR-NGHE-002.2: The teleoperation system shall introduce latency and control overhead not exceeding allocated cycle time budget.
FR-NGHE-002.3: Safety services shall not introduce blocking delays that degrade cycle time beyond acceptable margin.
FR-NGHE-002.4: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and report cycle time metrics via telemetry interface.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Optimize vehicle hydraulic/mechanical subsystems for speed; minimize teleoperation latency via low-latency network link; design safety services as non-blocking asynchronous checks.
DP-B: Implement predictive cycle time modeling and real-time adaptive control to dynamically adjust speed within safety envelope based on site conditions.

DP-C: Decompose cycle time into load, travel, dump phases; allocate time budgets to each phase; assign ownership to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules; instrument telemetry collection at phase boundaries.
DP-D: Centralize cycle time orchestration logic in a dedicated cycle manager; coordinate Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services via well-defined handoff interfaces.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C: Decompose cycle time into load, travel, dump phases; allocate time budgets to each phase; assign ownership to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules; instrument telemetry collection at phase boundaries.

potential_sources: Coupling between Vehicle phase timing and Teleoperation latency budget (mitigated by explicit phase allocation).
Coupling between Safety Services blocking behavior and overall cycle time (mitigated by non-blocking design principle).
Coupling between telemetry collection overhead and cycle time measurement accuracy (mitigated by phase-boundary instrumentation).
coupling_check.notes: DP-C is modular and decouples phase ownership, reducing cross-module dependencies. Explicit time budgets per phase enable independent optimization. Telemetry instrumentation at phase boundaries avoids intrusive measurement overhead.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Vehicle subsystems can achieve target load/travel/dump phase times independently.
Assumption: Teleoperation network link can sustain low-latency control within allocated budget.
Assumption: Safety services can be designed as non-blocking or with bounded latency.
Risk: If phase times are tightly coupled (e.g., dump phase depends on travel distance), phase-level budgeting may be insufficient; may require dynamic reallocation.
Risk: Site conditions (terrain, load mass) may cause phase time variance exceeding p95 target; requires margin analysis.
Risk: Telemetry collection itself may introduce measurement bias or overhead; requires validation.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-C to explicitly include: (1) phase time budget allocation with margin for site variability, (2) non-blocking safety service design principle, (3) telemetry instrumentation strategy (e.g., hardware timestamps at phase transitions to minimize software overhead).

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Top-level system integration module responsible for orchestrating cycle time performance across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services sub-modules. Owns cycle time KPI measurement, reporting, and phase-level time budget allocation.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Cycle time metrics (p95, phase durations, variance) reported to Site Manager and system monitoring dashboards.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Duty cycle definition and site conditions (terrain, load mass, distance) received from Site Manager to inform cycle time budgeting.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Safety events and constraints from Safety Services that may impact cycle time (e.g., emergency stop, reduced-speed mode).

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Low-latency communication with Teleoperation and Vehicle subsystems to coordinate phase transitions and monitor real-time cycle progress.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level container for system-wide KPI orchestration. Cycle time is a system-level concern requiring coordination across all sub-modules. High containment; minimal diffusion risk.

Vehicle owns phase execution (load, travel, dump) but not system-level cycle time orchestration or telemetry reporting. Assigning full DP to Vehicle would diffuse KPI ownership and complicate cross-module coordination.
Teleoperation owns latency control but not overall cycle time KPI. Assigning full DP to Teleoperation would obscure Vehicle and Safety Services contributions to cycle time.

Safety Services owns non-blocking design but not cycle time measurement or phase orchestration. Assigning full DP to Safety Services would be a poor fit; safety is a constraint, not the primary owner of KPI.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary because: (1) cycle time is a system-level KPI requiring visibility and coordination across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services; (2) phase-level time budget allocation and telemetry reporting are system-level concerns; (3) assigning to any sub-module would diffuse ownership and complicate verification.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution module may become a coordination bottleneck if cycle time orchestration logic is overly centralized; recommend clear delegation of phase execution to Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services with well-defined handoff interfaces.
Risk: Telemetry collection overhead at NGHE Solution level may introduce measurement bias; recommend hardware-level phase transition timestamps to minimize software overhead.
Risk: Site condition variability (terrain, load mass) may cause phase times to exceed budgets; recommend margin analysis and adaptive reallocation logic at NGHE Solution level.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify NGHE Solution responsibilities: (1) define phase handoff interfaces (e.g., load_complete, travel_complete, dump_complete signals), (2) specify telemetry collection points and timestamps, (3) document time budget allocation algorithm and margin strategy, (4) define escalation path if phase times exceed budget (e.g., alert Site Manager, trigger adaptive control).

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-C (phase decomposition with time budgets and telemetry instrumentation) directly addresses FR-NGHE-002 (achieve p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target). Phase-level budgeting enables independent optimization and measurement. Telemetry instrumentation supports verification. No functional gap identified.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion risk: Phase execution logic may be scattered across Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services without clear ownership of time budget compliance; mitigated by explicit phase handoff interfaces at NGHE Solution level.
Diffusion risk: Telemetry collection may be duplicated across modules if not centralized; mitigated by assigning telemetry reporting to NGHE Solution.

Diffusion risk: Safety Services non-blocking design principle may not be enforced if Safety Services module is not explicitly tasked with latency budgeting; recommend explicit DP allocation to Safety Services sub-module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution provides appropriate containment for system-level cycle time orchestration. Sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) will own phase execution and latency control; NGHE Solution owns coordination and KPI reporting. Clear interface boundaries required to avoid diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible via instrumented field trials with telemetry logging at phase boundaries. p95 cycle time can be computed from logged phase durations. Safety event correlation can be analyzed to ensure no safety compromise. Acceptance criteria (p95 ≤ CT_target) is measurable and objective.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: NGHE Solution owns system-level cycle time KPI and phase orchestration; Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services own phase execution and latency control. Interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined. Ownership boundaries are explicit.

recommended_revisions: Refine phase handoff interface definitions (e.g., load_complete, travel_complete, dump_complete signals with timestamps).
Document time budget allocation algorithm and margin strategy for site condition variability.
Specify telemetry collection points and timestamp precision requirements.
Define escalation path if phase times exceed budget (e.g., alert Site Manager, trigger adaptive control).
Allocate explicit DP to Safety Services sub-module for non-blocking design principle and latency budgeting.

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-002 is not atomic; it decomposes into four sub-FRs (load phase, travel phase, dump phase, telemetry measurement). DP-C (phase decomposition) is well-aligned with this decomposition and provides a clear path to sub-level design. NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary for system-level orchestration. Proceeding to next level of decomposition is justified to detail phase-level DPs and IMs.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-002.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within design envelope.
FR-NGHE-002.2: The teleoperation system shall introduce latency and control overhead not exceeding allocated cycle time budget.
FR-NGHE-002.3: Safety services shall not introduce blocking delays that degrade cycle time beyond acceptable margin.
FR-NGHE-002.4: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and report cycle time metrics via telemetry interface.

assumptions: Vehicle subsystems can achieve target load/travel/dump phase times independently.
Teleoperation network link can sustain low-latency control within allocated budget.
Safety services can be designed as non-blocking or with bounded latency.
Site conditions (terrain, load mass) can be characterized and incorporated into time budget allocation.
Telemetry collection overhead is negligible or can be minimized via hardware timestamps.
open_risks: Phase times may be tightly coupled (e.g., dump phase depends on travel distance), requiring dynamic reallocation.
Site condition variability may cause phase time variance exceeding p95 target; margin analysis required.
Telemetry collection may introduce measurement bias or overhead; validation required.
Safety Services non-blocking design principle may not be achievable for all safety checks; trade-off analysis required.
Teleoperation latency may be unpredictable due to network conditions; adaptive control or margin required.
evidence_needed: Phase time budget allocation algorithm with margin strategy.
Telemetry collection strategy and timestamp precision requirements.
Safety Services latency budget and non-blocking design validation.
Teleoperation latency characterization under representative network conditions.
Field trial data on phase time variance under site condition variability.
p95 cycle time measurement and acceptance criteria verification.
